task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Role-Based Access Control** (Level 8)

Build an API with role-based access. Users have roles (admin/editor/viewer). POST /api/articles creates articles (editors and admins only). DELETE /api/articles/:id deletes (admins only). GET /api/articles lists all (anyone with auth). Viewers can read but not create or delete.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for javascript backend

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique
  password, required
  role, required

create a
final_source:
/*
ROLE-BASED ACCESS CONTROL API

Users have roles: admin, editor, viewer
- Admins: read + create + delete articles
- Editors: read + create articles  
- Viewers: read articles only

ENDPOINTS:
- POST /api/articles (editors/admins only)
- DELETE /api/articles/:id (admins only)
- GET /api/articles (auth required)
- POST /auth/signup (auto-scaffolded)
- POST /auth/login (auto-scaffolded)
*/

build for web and javascript backend
allow signup and login
owner is 'admin@test.com'

database is local memory

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique
  password, required
  role, required

create an Articles table:
  title, required
  body
  author_id

# Role-based POST endpoint (create article)
when user calls POST /api/articles sending article:
  requires login
  user_role = current user's role
  not_admin = user_role is not 'admin'
  not_editor = user_role is not 'editor'
  is_restricted = not_admin and not_editor
  if is_restricted:
    send back { error: 'Only admins and editors can create articles' } with status 403
  
  article's author_id = current user's id
  saved = save article to Articles
  send back saved with status 201

# Role-based DELETE endpoint (admin only)
when user calls DELETE /api/articles/:id:
  requires login
  user_role = current user's role
  is_restricted = user_role is not 'admin'
  if is_restricted:
    send back { error: 'Only admins can delete articles' } with status 403
  
  delete from Articles with this id
  send back { success: true } with status 204

# GET endpoint (all authenticated users)
when user calls GET /api/articles:
  requires login
  articles = get all Articles
  send back articles

# Tests (HTTP endpoint tests)
test 'register admin':
  call POST /auth/signup sending { email: 'admin@test.com', password: 'pass1234', role: 'admin' }
  expect response status 201

test 'login with admin':
  call POST /auth/login sending { email: 'admin@test.com', password: 'pass1234' }
  expect response status 200

test 'articles without auth':
  call GET /api/articles
  expect response status 401
